steps.title: Recognize vertex form
steps.content:
The function $f(x) = (x+7)^2 + 4$ is in vertex form $f(x) = a(x-h)^2 + k$.
Rewriting to match this pattern: $f(x) = (x - (-7))^2 + 4$.
The vertex is at $(h, k) = (-7, 4)$.

steps.title: Find the minimum
steps.content:
Since $a = 1 > 0$, the parabola opens upward, making the vertex the minimum point.
Therefore, $f(x)$ reaches its minimum at $x = -7$.
